description_long: This object is a classic corded telephone featuring a rectangular base with a slightly raised, sloped surface. The handset is black and rests on the right side of the base, connected by a coiled white cord. The base is primarily white with a black lower section and is equipped with a numeric keypad and several additional function buttons, all clearly visible on the top surface. The keypad consists of standard number keys arranged in a grid, along with a few larger rectangular buttons likely for special functions such as redial or hold. The cradle for the handset is integrated into the right side of the base, providing a secure resting place. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of telephones found in offices or homes from the late 20th century.
description: A rectangular corded telephone with a black handset, white base, numeric keypad, and a coiled cord. The design is functional and typical of office or home desk phones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the black handset resting on the right side of the white base, with the keypad and function buttons visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the handset and the coiled cord extending outward from the base.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full keypad layout, function buttons, and the coiled cord attached to the handset.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the sloped surface of the base and the arrangement of the keypad and handset.
